topic: Perplexity AI search methodology, answer synthesis patterns

entries.a: Vane is a privacy-focused AI answering engine that runs entirely on your own hardware.

entries.q: What are the key features of Vane?
entries.a: Vane features include support for local LLMs and cloud providers, smart search modes, pick your sources, widgets, web search powered by SearxNG, image and video search, file uploads, and search specific domains.
Vane ensures privacy by running entirely on your own hardware and using SearxNG for web search, which keeps your identity private.

entries.q: What are the different search modes available in Vane?
entries.a: Vane offers three search modes: Speed Mode for quick answers, Balanced Mode for everyday searches, and Quality Mode for deep research.

entries.q: Can Vane be installed without Docker?
entries.a: Yes, Vane can be installed without Docker by cloning the repository, installing dependencies, building the application, and starting it manually.

entries.q: What is SearxNG and how is it used in Vane?
entries.a: SearxNG is a search engine that Vane uses for web search, which keeps your identity private. Vane can be used with a bundled SearxNG instance or with your own existing SearxNG instance.

entries.q: How do I get started with Vane using Docker?
entries.a: To get started with Vane using Docker, run the command 'docker run -d -p 3000:3000 -v vane-data:/home/vane/data --name vane itzcrazykns1337/vane:latest' and then configure your settings in the setup screen at http://localhost:3000.

entries.q: What are the system requirements for running Vane?
entries.a: The system requirements for running Vane include having Docker installed and running on your system, as well as ensuring that your SearxNG instance has JSON format enabled and Wolfram Alpha search engine enabled.
